Roam · Desktop — a re-housing, not a redesign

The phone column reads as a screenshot marooned on parchment. Showing the recommended direction only: an editorial masthead shell, carried across login → journeys → day. Same tokens as mobile — Playfair italic, DM Sans, parchment, ink, one sienna.

Why masthead: passes the airport-lounge test — italic Roam + hairline rule reads editorial-magazine the moment the laptop opens, which is the brand bar (Monocle × Condé Nast Traveller, not SaaS dashboard).

Container background: parchment everywhere(the existing mix of parchment-on-login and white-on-signed-in is what makes the desktop column look like a phone-screenshot frame — unifying on parchment is the single biggest fix).
Login — desktop
Pre-auth, so the shell question doesn't apply. Same parts as mobile (wordmark, photograph, caption, Continue), recomposed as a magazine spread: photo plate on the left, type-set on the right.
1 · Editorial spread
Kyoto · Higashiyama, autumn light
Roam
Trip planning simplified
Plan with care.
Travel with curiosity.
Day-by-day itineraries, the places you mean to keep, and a thinking partner along for the ride.
By continuing, you accept Roam's Terms and Privacy Policy.
Why a spread, not a centered column?

At desktop width a centered cartouche feels like a phone — which is exactly the problem. A 52/48 spread lets the photograph carry its own weight (no longer a tiny plate floating on parchment) and gives the type room to be a page, not a popup.

The caption stays small-caps, on the bottom edge of the photo — same museum-label treatment as mobile.

Ink button keeps full width but caps at 440px so it doesn't balloon — desktop CTAs that span half a viewport read as web-form.
Masthead shell — signed-in pages
Horizontal top bar: italic Roam wordmark, hairline, italic Playfair nav (Journeys · Atlas · Profile), avatar right. Inside a trip, a sub-bar carries the trip name and the Agenda/Plan/Map tabs that on mobile live in the bottom nav.

roam · map
Roam · Companion
Roam.

Duane Reade at 358 Broadway — as a pharmacy/clinic pair near the hotel, and you said to give it a shot. In hindsight I should have asked whether you wanted both or just one. If either feels like clutter, say the word and I’ll propose cutting it.

You.

How far is that Duane Reade from our hotel by walking?

Roam.

Let me check the exact address that came back from the lookup. Ah — that’s not the one I meant. The Duane Reade that resolved is at 35-06 Broadway in Astoria, Queens, which is across the East River, not a walk. That’s a query mishap on my end: I gave the server "Duane Reade 358 Broadway" and it locked onto the wrong Broadway.

The branch I had in mind is the Duane Reade at 358 Broadway in Manhattan (corner of Franklin), about a four-minute walk south of 11 Howard down Lafayette. Want me to cut the Astoria one and re-add the right Manhattan branch?

You.

Yeah sure.

Roam.

I’ll cut the Astoria mistake and propose the correct Manhattan branch.

You.

Approved — cutting 1 card.

Roam.

Cut Duane Reade. Nothing destroyed — held in case you want it back.

Think aloud with Roam…RETURN TO SEND

Trip settings — open question

Reached from inside a trip. How is undecided:

– A 4th tab next to Agenda · Plan · Map ("Settings")
– A small gear icon after the Map tab
– A "…" overflow menu (Trip settings · Archive · Delete)

I'd push for the overflow menu — keeps the tab row about the three primary surfaces, hides destructive actions one click away. But it's a real product call.
The shell — single masthead row

One bar, ~64px of chrome. Wordmark · Journeys breadcrumb · trip name · Agenda/Plan/Map tabs ······· search, avatar. The trip name acts as the breadcrumb — no second sub-bar, no redundant "Journeys" back link.

On the trips list (no trip context) the masthead is simpler still: wordmark · Journeys ········ search · avatar.

Active item picks up an ink underline. No sienna here — the single accent stays disciplined.
Journeys grid

Two-up grid + the existing hairline "Past journeys" divider. The cards are bigger because the imagery is what makes this page sing — at 390-wide they're stamps; at 540-wide they're plates.

Lede stays italic Playfair, "Brennan. New York awaits." — same copy, given a desktop scale (30px) that doesn't shout.
Day view — chevrons + "Day X of N" pill

One day at a time. Day-switching is two affordances:

‹ Thursday, 23 July ›  [Day 1 of 4 ⌄]  TORONTO → NEW YORK

Chevrons step prev/next (the 90% case). The "Day 1 of 4" pill is the visible click target — opens a Playfair-typeset list of the trip's days. The pill earns its space: it answers "how long is this trip again?" up front, and it's an obvious doorway (no hidden caret).

Scales: 3-night NYC or 13-night Japan reads the same — "Day 1 of 13" works identically.

Body is the two-column split — timeline left, map right. Sienna stays disciplined on "Talk this journey through".
What I'd lock

① Parchment everywhere. No more white container on signed-in pages. The mixed background is what makes the column read as a phone frame.

② Day view = timeline left, map right. The map is the desktop affordance the mobile app can't give. Promote it.

③ Numbered rows ↔ numbered pins. The two columns should read as one composition, not as a list beside a separate map.

④ Single sienna. Stays on "Talk this journey through", floated over the top of the map.

Open

– Trip cards: should past journeys show the photo at the same scale as upcoming, or stay as the small "monogram + meta" hairline rows (current mobile pattern)? I kept the small rows.
– When the Companion enters, does it land as a right-side panel on top of the two-column day view, or push the layout to three?
